author: praasz
title: Build job fails on artifact upload step for debian packages
signature: debian packages artifact upload step fails sub-second Build job|Infrastructure
key_errors: Step 37 'Upload OpenVINO debian packages' failed (conclusion: failure) within sub-second — all preceding build steps including 'Build Debian packages' (step 22) succeeded; artifact upload infrastructure failure suspected
root_cause: The 'Upload OpenVINO debian packages' step (step 37) in the Build / Build job failed almost instantly (< 1 second) despite the preceding 'Build Debian packages' step (step 22) completing successfully. The debian .deb files were confirmed present (step 40 'Prepare debian packages for storage on share' successfully ran mv *.deb deb/). The upload step uses ababushk/upload-artifact action with path $BUILD_DIR/*.deb and if-no-files-found: error. The sub-second failure strongly suggests a transient artifact service error or infrastructure issue rather than a missing file problem. Run attempt 2 was automatically triggered and is already in progress.
recommended_actions: Wait for run attempt 2 (job 87102048922) to complete — it was auto-triggered and is in progress.
If attempt 2 also fails at the same step, investigate the artifact upload service health.
Consider adding retry logic to the artifact upload step or switching to a more resilient upload mechanism.
